data.title: Wall Street giants plot $1 billion Solana treasury set to close in weeks shaking market
data.description:
Galaxy Digital, Multicoin Capital, and Jump Crypto are seeking about $1 billion to assemble a Solana treasury through a public company vehicle, according to Bloomberg, with Cantor Fitzgerald engaged as lead banker and a takeover of a listed entity contemplated for the structure.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/wall-street-giants-plot-1-billion-solana-treasury-as-dex-volumes-surge/

data.title: Grayscale Files for Avalanche ETF with SEC
data.description: According to PANews, Grayscale has submitted an S-1 filing for an Avalanche ETF with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), as per the SEC's official website. This move marks another step in Grayscale's efforts to expand its offerings in the cryptocurrency investment space.

data.title: FLock.io and HKGAI Collaborate on AI Solutions for Public Sector
data.description:
According to Foresight News, decentralized AI training platform FLock.io has entered into a strategic partnership with the Hong Kong Generative AI Research Center (HKGAI). The collaboration focuses on developing next-generation AI solutions for government and public sectors, utilizing federated learning technology to build a compliant, reliable, and privacy-first intelligent governance system. This initiative aims to enhance the precision and efficiency of public services.

Established in October 2023, HKGAI is the only AI project supported by the Hong Kong government under the InnoHK innovation program, led by the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology. This partnership marks a significant step by the Hong Kong government in advancing the application of decentralized AI technologies. Both parties will continue to explore pathways for technology integration to construct decentralized AI infrastructure for the public sector, facilitating efficiency improvements and the implementation of innovative applications.

data.title: Solana Leads in Active Addresses Among Top Blockchains
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Nansen reveals that Solana has emerged as the leading blockchain in terms of active addresses over the past week. Solana recorded 21.182 million active addresses, surpassing other major blockchains. Following Solana, BNB Chain registered 10.909 million active addresses, while Tron had 6.664 million. Base and Sei rounded out the top five with 6.521 million and 4.96 million active addresses, respectively.

data.title: This statistic hints at extended Bitcoin losses in the next 7 days
data.description:
As Bitcoin (BTC) faces the renewed threat of plunging below the $110,000 support amid a broader market sell-off, historical data suggests the asset may be destined for more losses in September. Since 2015, Bitcoin has managed only a 40% win rate in September, with an average return of -2.5%, according to data from charting platform TrendSpider shared on August 25. Bitcoin seasonality chart
source: https://finbold.com/this-statistic-hints-at-extended-bitcoin-losses-in-the-next-7-days/

data.title: Binance Market Update (2025-08-25)
data.description:
The global cryptocurrency market cap now stands at $3.98T, down by -0.41% over the last day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $110,680 and $115,061 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $111,478, down by -2.92%.

Most major cryptocurrencies by market cap are trading lower. Market outperformers include ONT, ONG, and IOST, up by 52%, 21%, and 16%, respectively.

Digital Asset Firms Aim to Raise $1 Billion for Solana Acquisition

Ethereum's Recent Outperformance Over Bitcoin May Be Short-Lived

Citigroup Updates U.S. Treasury Yield Forecasts for Year-End
